sources.label: AMETEK press release - AMETEK Completes Acquisition of FARO Technologies (Jul 21, 2025; $44.00/share cash, ~$920M; Nasdaq delisting)
PR Newswire - AMETEK Completes Acquisition of FARO Technologies
SEC 8-K exhibit - FARO Technologies merger completion

verdicts.rankRationale: FARO ceased to exist as a public equity when AMETEK closed its $44.00/share (~$920M) cash acquisition on July 21, 2025 and the stock was delisted from Nasdaq; a non-tradeable ticker carries zero residual alpha on a latent-nodes ranking and should be removed entirely. This reiterates yesterday's 'remove' verdict, which the page has not yet executed (still shown at rank 38).
verdicts.bull: The thesis itself was vindicated: a strategic acquirer paid cash for exactly the 3D-capture/digital-twin bottleneck asset the page identified, folding it into AMETEK's Ultra Precision Technologies division alongside Creaform and Virtek.
verdicts.neutral: Investors wanting the exposure now own it only through AMETEK (NYSE: AME), where FARO's ~$340M of sales is a small slice of a large diversified instruments company - a different, far more diluted bet.
verdicts.bear: Keeping a delisted ticker on the list is dead weight that misstates the page's investable universe; null price/market-cap data confirms there is no security left to rank.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: None - the equity no longer trades; the only successor catalyst is AMETEK's integration of FARO into its metrology portfolio.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Listing hygiene risk: stale, non-investable entries erode the credibility of the alpha ranking rather than any market risk.
